verification_gate: Every user-visible capability resolves through stable governed APIs; business logic and authority stay outside replaceable clients; offline, error, rollback, and proof behavior are specified.

api_families_match_goal_terms.evidence: The seven families map one-to-one onto the seven API kinds named in the EXPERIENCE-001 goal: application, state, command, event, plugin, policy, data.

clause_1_every_user_visible_capability_r.evidence: user_visible_capabilities enumerates 19 capabilities (view swarm status, semantic codebase search, knowledge-graph browse, AI chat, provider select, ingestion trigger+monitor, materialize+build release, temporal forecast, task/bead board, task handoff, prompt registry, policy/MinCut/merge verdicts, home/network control, storage health/recover, plugin list/invoke, secret-reference management, meta orchestration view, evidence-corpus browse, hardware inventory). Each names one or more of the 7 API families and exactly one governing bounded context + published contract from CONSOLIDATE-001. api_family_contract_coverage proves the inverse coverage: all 34 consumed governed contracts (of the 36 CONSOLIDATE-001 contracts; 2 BC-UI-PRESENTATION contracts define the client role) are fronted by an API family — 27 user-visible, 7 internal-only — so no user-visible capability lacks a governed API and no governed contract is unreachable.

clause_2_business_logic_and_authority_st.evidence: application_boundary.client_contract enumerates clients_may (render governed state/data, dispatch typed commands, subscribe to events, request policy verdicts, list/invoke plugins, read lifecycle/health, display proofs) vs clients_may_not (mutate durable state directly, embed business rules, become a second durable writer, hold raw secrets, bypass policy, re-derive authority locally). Every one of the 7 api_families declares an authority_location inside a Rust-native bounded context: API-COMMAND routes each mutation into the OWNING context under its STORE-001 authority and API-POLICY re-checks so a client cannot bypass; API-DATA writes redirect to API-COMMAND. The six workspaces + /settings are declared navigation groupings with no authority. Any client (Vue3+Tauri now; desktop/mobile/glasses/ambient later) is swappable without moving logic.

authority_locations.secrets / provenance: OS keyring + committed pins (BC-SECRET) — client holds references only
authority_locations.policy / verification: BC-INTEGRITY — verdicts only, no durable state

clause_3_offline_error_rollback_and_proo.offline: redb application-level WAL + envctl forward-only flush on reconnect; reads degrade to redb-cached snapshots with a stale marker; durable writes queue and forward-flush; the client never becomes a durable authority (STORE-001 single-durable-writer invariant).
clause_3_offline_error_rollback_and_proo.error: Every API returns a typed Result; failures surface as calm second-person present-tense sentence-case strings (design voice), e.g. 'LifeOS couldn't reach the AI provider right now — try again.' and 'LifeOS couldn't initialize storage at <path> — see logs.'; no raw stack traces reach the client.
clause_3_offline_error_rollback_and_proo.rollback: Owning-context mechanisms — COW staging-branch discard (BC-PERSIST/BC-ORCHESTRATION), idempotent replayable migration re-run (BC-BRIDGE db_migrate), BlobStore SHA-256 parity reversal with redb retained until green (STORE-001 verified cutover), meta git snapshot restore (BC-CONTROL-PLANE); each Command API result carries a rollback_handle; the client requests, the core executes.
clause_3_offline_error_rollback_and_proo.proof: SHAKE256 Cryptographic Witness Chains rooted in PostgreSQL source vectors + causal-graph verification reject rootless claims; proof_ref on every mutation; Event API streams proof-carrying verdicts (MinCut isolation, merge-gate result); a client cannot forge a proof.
clause_3_offline_error_rollback_and_proo.evidence: cross_cutting_behavior specifies all four globally, and each of the 7 api_families entries also carries its own offline_behavior/error_behavior/rollback_behavior/proof_behavior fields.

store_001_alignment.note: The API boundary enforces STORE-001 by construction — no API family lets a client mutate durable state or become a durable writer; PostgreSQL + RuVector remains the single durable owner; redb is the transient/WAL tier; durable-class cutover is server-side via verified BlobStore SHA-256 parity only.

proof_summary: EXPERIENCE-001 defines the stable Rust-native LifeOS application boundary and its seven-family governed API (application, state, command, event, plugin, policy, data), exactly matching the seven API kinds named in the task goal. The design artifact (generated/task_families/EXPERIENCE-001.json) fronts all 34 consumed governed contracts from CONSOLIDATE-001's 15 bounded contexts (36 published contracts; the 2 BC-UI-PRESENTATION contracts define the client role) and maps 19 enumerated user-visible capabilities — swarm status, semantic codebase search, knowledge-graph browse, AI chat/completion, provider selection, ingestion, materialize/build release, temporal forecast, task/bead board, task handoff, prompt registry, policy/MinCut/merge verdicts, home/network control, storage health, plugin list/invoke, secret-reference management, meta orchestration, evidence browse, and hardware inventory — each to one or more API families and exactly one governing context + contract, satisfying gate clause 1. Gate clause 2 holds because every API family names an authority_location inside a Rust-native bounded context: the Command API routes mutations into the owning context and the Policy API re-checks so a client cannot bypass; clients may only render, dispatch, subscribe, request verdicts, and display proofs, and the six workspaces + settings are authority-free navigation groupings — preserving STORE-001's single-durable-writer invariant (BC-UI-PRESENTATION and all future clients own no durable macro-state; PostgreSQL + RuVector stays the sole durable writer, redb the transient/WAL tier). Gate clause 3 holds because offline (redb WAL + envctl forward-only flush), error (typed Results + calm design-voice strings), rollback (COW discard, idempotent migration, SHA-256 parity reversal, meta snapshot restore — core-executed on client request), and proof (SHAKE256 witness chains rooted in PostgreSQL vectors + causal-graph verification + proof_ref on every mutation) are specified both globally and per API family. No product execution, no application-source mutation, no UI rewrite, no deployment, and no secrets access occurred — planning artifacts only.
